analysis.overall_assessment: The image shows a single leaf that is unhealthy, with brown spots on it.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Monitor and regulate air quality around tea plantations

immediate_actions: Separate unhealthy leaves from healthy ones during processing
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Sort leaves based on color and texture, discarding any with visible spots or discoloration
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Use a gentle drying method to preserve the quality of remaining leaves
quality_preservation: Store remaining leaves in a cool, dark place away from direct sunlight

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: The rest of the leaf can be used for tea processing
alternative_uses: Use unhealthy leaves in composting or as a natural fertilizer
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Compost the unhealthy leaves separately from healthy ones to prevent contamination
prevention_measures: Implement regular monitoring of air quality around tea plantations

economic_impact.estimated_loss_percentage: 10% loss in leaf yield due to unhealthy leaves
cost_saving_opportunities: Invest in air quality monitoring and control systems
value_recovery_methods: Compost or use as natural fertilizer for soil improvement

priority_actions: Monitor and regulate air quality around tea plantations
Separate unhealthy leaves from healthy ones during processing
recommendations.timeline: Begin implementing measures within 1 month, monitor results for the next 6 months
